sections.subtitle: Financije u braku: Tko je pravi lider?
sections.screenshot_description: Voditeljica postavlja pitanje o upravljanju novcem u braku, sugerirajući da sposobnost, a ne spol, treba određivati tko vodi financije.
sections.content: Voditeljica podcasta "Redefinicija" započinje razgovor važnom temom upravljanja financijama unutar partnerskih odnosa. Ističe kako se često pogrešno pretpostavlja da je muškarac, kao "glava obitelji", automatski i najbolji upravitelj novcem. Međutim, naglašava da ako je žena sposobnija u tom području, ona bi trebala preuzeti tu ulogu. Upozorava da inzistiranje na tradicionalnim ulogama, unatoč lošem upravljanju jednog partnera, može dovesti do ozbiljnih problema u vezi. Ključno je prepoznati tko je kompetentniji i prepustiti mu vođenje financija radi dobrobiti oboje.

magisterium.assessment: Ova sekcija je u velikoj mjeri usklađena s katoličkim naukom jer naglašava zajedničko dobro supružnika, njihovu jednaku dostojanstvenost i potrebu za razboritim upravljanjem obiteljskim dobrima.
concerns: Potrebno je paziti da se uloga 'glave obitelji' ne svede isključivo na financijsku moć ili tehničko upravljanje, već da se razumije kao služenje u ljubavi.
Naglasak na kompetenciji ne smije zasjeniti sakramentalnu narav braka kao zajednice osoba u kojoj se odluke donose zajedničkim vijećanjem.
magisterium.enrichment: Katolički nauk naučava da su muž i žena jednaki u dostojanstvu te da je obitelj zajednica koja zahtijeva 'zajedničko vijećanje supružnika' o svim važnim pitanjima. Upravljanje imovinom u braku promatra se kao služenje (stewardship) Božjoj providnosti za dobrobit obitelji i društva.
magisterium.assessment_en: This section is largely aligned with Catholic teaching because it emphasizes the common good of the spouses, their equal dignity, and the need for prudent management of family assets.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Catholic teaching teaches that husband and wife are equal in dignity and that the family is a community which requires 'common counsel of spouses' on all important matters. The management of property in marriage is viewed as service (stewardship) to God's providence for the well-being of the family and society.
concerns_en: It is necessary to ensure that the role of the 'head of the family' is not reduced exclusively to financial power or technical management, but rather understood as service in love.
The emphasis on competence must not overshadow the sacramental nature of marriage as a community of persons in which decisions are made by common deliberation.

sections.subtitle_en: Finances in marriage: Who is the true leader?
sections.screenshot_description_en: The hostess asks a question about managing money in marriage, suggesting that ability, and not gender, should determine who manages finances.
sections.content_en: The host of the podcast "Redefinicija" begins the conversation with an important topic of financial management within partner relationships. She points out how it is often wrongly assumed that the man, as the "head of the family," is automatically also the best money manager. However, she emphasizes that if the woman is more capable in that area, she should take on that role. She warns that insisting on traditional roles, despite one partner's poor management, can lead to serious problems in the relationship. It is crucial to recognize who is more competent and entrust them with financial management for the well-being of both.

sections.subtitle: Marko Matijašević: Predsjednik udruge EHO i vizionar
sections.screenshot_description: Voditelj predstavlja Marka Matijaševića i udrugu EHO.
sections.content: Voditelj podcasta srdačno pozdravlja Marka Matijaševića, predsjednika udruge EHO, kao posebnog gosta u novoj epizodi "Redefinicije". Najavljuje razgovor o raznim zanimljivim projektima i aktivnostima kojima se udruga EHO bavi, obećavajući slušateljima uvid u njihov rad i misiju.

magisterium.assessment: Opisana sekcija u potpunosti je usklađena s katoličkim naukom jer promiče vodstvo kao služenje i bavi se ključnim pitanjima braka i obitelji.
magisterium.enrichment: Katolička tradicija definira autoritet isključivo kao 'diakoniu' ili služenje, po uzoru na Krista koji je došao služiti, a ne biti služen. U kontekstu braka, Crkva naučava da su supružnici jedno drugome primarna služba te da kroz međusobno darivanje i nadvladavanje izazova sudjeluju u Kristovoj ljubavi prema Crkvi.
magisterium.assessment_en: The described section is fully aligned with Catholic teaching because it promotes leadership as service and addresses key issues of marriage and family.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Catholic tradition defines authority exclusively as 'diakonia' or service, following the example of Christ who came to serve, and not to be served. In the context of marriage, the Church teaches that spouses are each other's primary service and that through mutual self-giving and overcoming challenges they participate in Christ's love for the Church.

sections.subtitle_en: Marko Matijašević: President of the EHO association and visionary
sections.screenshot_description_en: The host introduces Marko Matijašević and the EHO association.
sections.content_en: The podcast host cordially greets Marko Matijašević, the president of the EHO association, as a special guest in the new episode of "Redefinicije". He announces a discussion about various interesting projects and activities that the EHO association is involved in, promising listeners insight into their work and mission.

sections.subtitle: Identitet izvan profesije: Marko Matijašević o prioritetima
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako sebe definira prvenstveno kao muža i oca.
sections.content: Na pitanje voditelja o svom identitetu, Marko Matijašević ističe da se prvenstveno definira kao **muž i otac**, a tek potom kao teolog i voditelj. Naglašava da je teško razdvojiti ono što radimo od našeg identiteta, ali upozorava na zamku poistovjećivanja vlastite vrijednosti isključivo s profesijom. Njegov odgovor odražava duboko promišljanje o prioritetima i važnosti obiteljskih uloga u definiranju sebe.

magisterium.assessment: Sekcija je u potpunosti usklađena s katoličkim naukom jer ispravno postavlja hijerarhiju identiteta, dajući prednost sakramentalnim i obiteljskim ulogama nad profesionalnim postignućima.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva uči da obitelj pronalazi svoj identitet i misiju u Božjem planu kao 'zajednica života i ljubavi', što prethodi svakom profesionalnom djelovanju. Iako je rad važan za razvoj osobe, on se ne smije pretvoriti u krajnji cilj koji bi zasjenio dostojanstvo osobe stvorene na sliku Božju i njezino primarno zvanje u obitelji.
magisterium.assessment_en: The section is fully aligned with Catholic teaching because it correctly establishes the hierarchy of identity, giving priority to sacramental and family roles over professional achievements.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church teaches that the family finds its identity and mission in God's plan as a 'community of life and love,' which precedes every professional activity. Although work is important for the development of the person, it must not turn into an ultimate goal that would overshadow the dignity of the person created in God's image and their primary vocation in the family.

sections.subtitle_en: Identity outside of profession: Marko Matijašević about priorities
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how he primarily defines himself as a husband and father.
sections.content_en: To the host's question about his identity, Marko Matijašević emphasizes that he primarily defines himself as a **husband and father**, and only then as a theologian and leader. He emphasizes that it is difficult to separate what we do from our identity, but warns against the trap of identifying one's own worth exclusively with one's profession. His answer reflects a deep reflection on priorities and the importance of family roles in defining oneself.

sections.subtitle: EHO: Evangelizacija i praktična vjera inspirirana Tobijinom knjigom
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o misiji udruge EHO i inspiraciji iz Tobijine knjige.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević objašnjava da je temeljna svrha udruge EHO **evangelizacija i odgoj vjernika**, s fokusom na to kako vjernici žive svoja uvjerenja u svakodnevnom životu. Rad udruge inspiriran je Tobijinom knjigom, posebno savjetima koje je Tobija stariji dao svom sinu. Ti savjeti obuhvaćaju pet ključnih područja:
- Briga za majku i obiteljske odnose.
- Odnos prema molitvi i Bogu.
- Odnos prema novcu i davanje sugrađanima.
- Odnos prema radnicima (liderstvo i poduzetništvo).
- Partnerski odnosi (izbor supruge iz vlastitog roda).
Ovi principi služe kao temelj za brojne programe udruge EHO.

magisterium.assessment: Program udruge EHO snažno je utemeljen na biblijskim načelima Knjige o Tobiji i u potpunosti odražava katolički nauk o obitelji, molitvi, socijalnoj pravednosti i milostinji. Jedini element koji zahtijeva pažljivu teološku kontekstualizaciju je 'izbor supruge iz vlastitog roda', koji se u kršćanskom smislu treba tumačiti kao jedinstvo u vjeri, a ne kao etnička isključivost.
concerns: Potrebno je jasno naglasiti da se starozavjetni poticaj na ženidbu unutar roda (Tob 4,12) u suvremenom katoličkom kontekstu odnosi na zajedničku vjeru i duhovne vrijednosti supružnika, a ne na krvno srodstvo ili nacionalnu pripadnost.
Važno je osigurati da 'liderstvo prema radnicima' ne ostane samo na razini učinkovitosti, već da se temelji na dostojanstvu ljudske osobe kao slike Božje.
magisterium.enrichment: Udruga može obogatiti rad integracijom nauka o sakramentalnosti braka i teologiji tijela, čime bi se starozavjetni primjeri uzdigli na razinu novozavjetnog otajstva Krista i Crkve. Naglašavanje uloge Duha Svetoga kao onoga koji poučava molitvi produbilo bi duhovnu dimenziju njihovih pet područja.
magisterium.assessment_en: The program of the EHO association is strongly founded on the biblical principles of the Book of Tobit and fully reflects Catholic teaching on family, prayer, social justice, and almsgiving. The only element that requires careful theological contextualization is 'the choice of a wife from one's own kin', which in the Christian sense should be interpreted as unity in faith, and not as ethnic exclusivity.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The association can enrich its work by integrating the teaching on the sacramentality of marriage and the theology of the body, whereby Old Testament examples would be elevated to the level of the New Testament mystery of Christ and the Church. Emphasizing the role of the Holy Spirit as the one who teaches prayer would deepen the spiritual dimension of their five areas.
concerns_en: It is necessary to clearly emphasize that the Old Testament encouragement for marriage within the kin (Tob 4,12) in the contemporary Catholic context refers to the common faith and spiritual values of the spouses, and not to blood relation or national affiliation.
It is important to ensure that 'leadership towards workers' does not remain only at the level of efficiency, but that it is based on the dignity of the human person as an image of God.

sections.subtitle_en: EHO: Evangelization and practical faith inspired by the Book of Tobit
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević talks about the mission of the EHO association and inspiration from the Book of Tobit.
sections.content_en:
Marko Matijašević explains that the fundamental purpose of the EHO association is **evangelization and education of believers**, with a focus on how believers live their convictions in everyday life. The work of the association is inspired by the Book of Tobit, especially the advice that Tobit the elder gave to his son. These pieces of advice encompass five key areas:
- Care for mother and family relationships.
- Relationship with prayer and God.
- Relationship with money and giving to fellow citizens.
- Relationship with workers (leadership and entrepreneurship).
- Partner relationships (choice of wife from one's own kin).
These principles serve as a foundation for numerous programs of the EHO association.

sections.subtitle: Od Echo Kids do EHO konferencija: Širok spektar programa za sve uzraste
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević detaljnije opisuje projekte udruge EHO, uključujući Echo Kids i godišnje konferencije.
sections.content:
Udruga EHO razvila je preko deset aktivnih programa, svi usmjereni na primjenu vjere u svakodnevnom životu – od partnerskih odnosa i financija do posla i duhovnosti. Marko Matijašević s posebnim entuzijazmom ističe projekt **Echo Kids**, namijenjen djeci od tri do šest godina, koji je bio njegov dugogodišnji san.
Kao "main event" godine, izdvaja se **EHO konferencija**, koja se održava svake godine (sljedeća će biti 14. ili 15. po redu, započeta 2014. godine). Naziv "EHO" simbolizira ideju da sve što rade treba "odjeknuti". Osim glavne konferencije, udruga organizira i "Budi" konferenciju za srednjoškolce i studente, te konferenciju za žene, neprestano šireći svoj doseg i utjecaj.

magisterium.assessment: Programi udruge EHO, uključujući Echo Kids, konferencije za mlade 'Budi' i konferencije za žene, pokazuju visoku razinu usklađenosti s katoličkim naukom o cjelovitoj formaciji vjernika i pozivu Crkve na novu evangelizaciju.
concerns: Potrebno je osigurati da se u programima za djecu (Echo Kids) ne zanemari uloga roditelja kao 'prvih glasnika vjere'.
Kod konferencija za žene važno je jasno komunicirati katoličko učenje o komplementarnosti i specifičnim ulogama u Crkvi.
magisterium.enrichment: Za daljnji razvoj programa preporučuje se snažnije integriranje biblijske meditacije i liturgijske molitve. Konferencije za studente mogu se obogatiti sadržajima koji promiču dijalog između vjere i znanosti.
magisterium.assessment_en: Programs of the EHO association, including Echo Kids, 'Budi' youth conferences, and women's conferences, show a high level of alignment with Catholic teaching on the integral formation of believers and the Church's call to new evangelization.
magisterium.enrichment_en: For further development of the program, stronger integration of biblical meditation and liturgical prayer is recommended. Conferences for students can be enriched with content that promotes dialogue between faith and science.
concerns_en: It is necessary to ensure that in programs for children (Echo Kids) the role of parents as 'first heralds of faith' is not neglected.
At conferences for women, it is important to clearly communicate Catholic teaching about complementarity and specific roles in the Church.

sections.subtitle_en: From Echo Kids to EHO conferences: Wide spectrum of programs for all ages
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević describes in more detail the projects of the EHO association, including Echo Kids and annual conferences.
sections.content_en: The EHO Association has developed over ten active programs, all aimed at the application of faith in everyday life – from partner relationships and finances to work and spirituality. Marko Matijašević with special enthusiasm highlights the project **Echo Kids**, intended for children aged three to six years, which was his long-standing dream. As the "main event" of the year, the **EHO conference** stands out, which is held every year (the next one will be the 14th or 15th in order, started in 2014). The name "EHO" symbolizes the idea that everything they do should "resound". Besides the main conference, the association also organizes the "Budi" conference for high school students and university students, and a conference for women, constantly expanding its reach and influence.

sections.subtitle: Muško liderstvo i samorazvoj: Kad je lider dobar, svi dobivaju
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o važnosti muškog liderstva i osobnog razvoja, motiviranog djecom.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević naglašava ključnu ulogu muškog liderstva i neprestanog osobnog razvoja. Citirajući slogan s GLS konferencije, "Kad lider vodi, kad je lider dobar, svi dobivaju", Marko to primjenjuje na vlastiti život i obitelj. Njegova supruga Mirena jednom je prilikom istaknula da se isplati moliti za njegove blagoslove, jer "kad tebi dobro ide, onda svi imamo koristi od toga".
Marko ističe da je najgore za bilo koju osobu stagnirati i smatrati da nema prostora za napredak. Kao figure autoriteta u obitelji, muškarci se moraju neprestano propitivati i raditi na sebi. Njegovi sinovi su mu najveća motivacija za samorazvoj, jer je shvatio da djeca upijaju ono što roditelji **jesu**, a ne samo ono što govore. Primjerice, kada je njegov sin ponovio naviku koju Marko nije želio, to ga je potaknulo na dublje preispitivanje vlastitih obrazaca ponašanja.

magisterium.assessment: Tvrdnja je u potpunosti usklađena s katoličkim naukom koji naglašava roditeljski primjer kao temeljni odgojni alat i očinsku odgovornost za duhovno vodstvo obitelji.
magisterium.enrichment: Crkva uči da otac stoji na Božjem mjestu u obitelji, pozvan na 'kraljevsko svećeništvo' kroz svjedočanstvo svetog života, samoodricanje i neprestano prenošenje evanđelja vlastitim primjerom.
magisterium.assessment_en: The statement is fully aligned with Catholic teaching which emphasizes parental example as a fundamental educational tool and paternal responsibility for the spiritual leadership of the family.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Church teaches that the father stands in God's place in the family, called to 'royal priesthood' through the witness of a holy life, self-denial, and the constant transmission of the Gospel by his own example.

sections.subtitle_en: Male leadership and self-development: When the leader is good, everyone benefits
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about the importance of male leadership and personal development, motivated by children.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević emphasizes the key role of male leadership and continuous personal development. Citing the slogan from the GLS conference, "When a leader leads, when a leader is good, everyone benefits," Marko applies this to his own life and family. His wife Mirena once pointed out that it is worth praying for his blessings, because "when things go well for you, then we all benefit from it." Marko emphasizes that the worst thing for any person is to stagnate and believe that there is no room for improvement. As figures of authority in the family, men must constantly question themselves and work on themselves. His sons are his greatest motivation for self-development, because he realized that children absorb what parents **are**, and not just what they say. For example, when his son repeated a habit that Marko did not want, it prompted him to a deeper re-examination of his own behavior patterns.

sections.subtitle: Strah od odbijanja: Zašto muškarci oklijevaju u vezama?
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević analizira strah od odbijanja kao ključni faktor pasivnosti muškaraca u vezama.
sections.content: Voditelj primjećuje trend sve manje proaktivnosti muškaraca u započinjanju veza, na što Marko Matijašević odgovara da je temeljni problem **strah od odbijanja i odbacivanja**. Za muškarca, neuspjeh u pokušaju udvaranja može biti duboko ponižavajuće iskustvo. Ako se taj osjećaj ne obradi ispravno, može dovesti do potpunog zatvaranja i izbjegavanja novih pokušaja, ili pak do suprotnog ekstrema – neselektivnog pristupa. Marko smatra da je to dio šireg problema u društvu, gdje se izbjegava preuzimanje odgovornosti, ne samo u romantičnim odnosima, već i u drugim životnim situacijama.

magisterium.assessment: Procjena o strahu od odbijanja kao uzroku oklijevanja muškaraca i povezanosti s izbjegavanjem odgovornosti visoko je usklađena s katoličkim naukom; Crkva ovaj fenomen tumači kao krizu povjerenja u Božju providnost i strah od samodarivanja.
concerns: Psihološki uzroci ne smiju potpuno dokinuti osobnu moralnu odgovornost; grijeh je uvijek osobni čin.
Izbjeći 'Adamov sindrom' - krivnju prebacivati na žene ili društvene promjene.
magisterium.enrichment: Strah od odbijanja zapravo je strah od 'smrti' vlastitog ega. Kristovo ozdravljenje rana omogućuje muškarcu da postane 'čovjek zajedništva'. Odgovornost se vidi kao 'suodgovornost' koja zahtijeva vrline razboritosti, umjerenosti i hrabrosti.
magisterium.assessment_en: The assessment of the fear of rejection as the cause of men's hesitation and its connection with avoiding responsibility is highly aligned with Catholic teaching; the Church interprets this phenomenon as a crisis of trust in God's providence and a fear of self-giving.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Fear of rejection is actually the fear of the 'death' of one's own ego. Christ's healing of wounds enables a man to become 'a man of communion'. Responsibility is seen as 'co-responsibility' which requires the virtues of prudence, temperance, and courage.
concerns_en: Psychological causes must not completely abolish personal moral responsibility; sin is always a personal act.
Avoid 'Adam's syndrome' - shifting blame onto women or social changes.

sections.subtitle_en: Fear of rejection: Why do men hesitate in relationships?
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević analyzes the fear of rejection as a key factor in men's passivity in relationships.
sections.content_en: The host notices a trend of decreasing proactivity of men in initiating relationships, to which Marko Matijašević responds that the fundamental problem is **fear of rejection and dismissal**. For a man, failure in an attempt at courtship can be a deeply humiliating experience. If that feeling is not processed correctly, it can lead to complete withdrawal and avoidance of new attempts, or to the opposite extreme – a non-selective approach. Marko believes that this is part of a broader problem in society, where taking responsibility is avoided, not only in romantic relationships, but also in other life situations.

sections.subtitle: Pet godina upornosti: Markova priča o osvajanju Mirene
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević dijeli osobno svjedočanstvo o dugogodišnjem osvajanju svoje supruge Mirene.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević dijeli inspirativnu priču o svojoj upornosti u osvajanju supruge Mirene. Iako je proces trajao pet godina, on to ne vidi kao neuspjeh, već kao dokaz svoje ustrajnosti. S 19 godina, Marko je otvoreno izjavljivao svoje namjere, no Mirena se bojala njegove posvećenosti pozivu koji je uključivao financijsku nesigurnost.
Nakon četiri godine, Marko je privremeno odustao i ušao u drugu vezu. Zanimljivo, Mirena je u tom periodu imala dva sna u kojima joj je Bog jasno pokazao da je Marko "onaj pravi", ali joj je rečeno da mu to ne smije reći. Nakon što je Marko prekinuo svoju vezu, ponovno je odlučio pokušati s Mirenom. Njegova smirena reakcija na njezinu početnu nesigurnost bila je ključna. Danas su zajedno deset godina, od čega osam u braku, a Mirena mu je postala velika podrška, posebno u izazovnim razdobljima poput pandemije COVID-19, kada je Marko bio suočen s depresijom zbog neizvjesnosti posla.

magisterium.assessment: Markova i Mirenina priča u velikoj mjeri odražava katoličko shvaćanje braka kao cjeloživotnog poziva koji se temelji na čvrstoj i realnoj odluci. Markova petogodišnja ustrajnost u udvaranju može se promatrati kao dio pedagogije ljubavi koja pomaže mladima da sazriju. Mirenini snovi spadaju u privatne objave koje, iako ne pripadaju pologu vjere, mogu biti pomoć vjeri.
concerns: Markovo privremeno odustajanje i ulazak u drugu vezu može se promatrati kao trenutak ljudske slabosti.
Oslanjanje isključivo na snove za odluke zahtijeva oprez; privatne objave zahtijevaju razborito prianjanje.
Zaručnici se trebaju pripremati za brak kroz učenje vjernosti i čistoću.
magisterium.enrichment: Par se potiče da svoju povijest promatra kroz prizmu Božje vjerne ljubavi koja nadilazi ljudske napore. Važno je redovito sudjelovanje u sakramentalnom životu, posebno u sakramentu pomirenja. Njihovo iskustvo može poslužiti kao svjedočanstvo o važnosti strpljenja i povjerenja u Božju providnost.
magisterium.assessment_en: Marko's and Mirena's story largely reflects the Catholic understanding of marriage as a lifelong vocation based on a firm and realistic decision. Marko's five-year perseverance in courtship can be seen as part of the pedagogy of love that helps young people to mature. Mirena's dreams belong to private revelations which, although they do not belong to the deposit of faith, can be an aid to faith.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The couple is encouraged to view their history through the prism of God's faithful love which transcends human efforts. It is important to regularly participate in sacramental life, especially in the sacrament of reconciliation. Their experience can serve as a testimony to the importance of patience and trust in God's providence.
concerns_en: Mark's temporary giving up and entering into another relationship can be viewed as a moment of human weakness.
Relying exclusively on dreams for decisions requires caution; private revelations require prudent adherence.
Fiancés should prepare themselves for marriage through learning fidelity and purity.

sections.subtitle_en: Five years of perseverance: Marko's story about conquering Mirena
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević shares a personal testimony about the long-term winning over of his wife Mirena.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević shares an inspiring story about his persistence in winning over his wife Mirena. Although the process lasted five years, he does not see it as a failure, but as proof of his perseverance. At 19 years old, Marko openly declared his intentions, but Mirena was afraid of his dedication to a calling that involved financial insecurity. After four years, Marko temporarily gave up and entered another relationship. Interestingly, during that period, Mirena had two dreams in which God clearly showed her that Marko was "the one," but she was told not to tell him. After Marko ended his relationship, he decided to try again with Mirena. His calm reaction to her initial insecurity was crucial. Today they have been together for ten years, eight of which in marriage, and Mirena has become a great support to him, especially during challenging periods like the COVID-19 pandemic, when Marko was faced with depression due to job uncertainty.

sections.subtitle: Lideri u braku: Kako Marko i Mirena žongliraju karijeru i obitelj
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević opisuje izazove usklađivanja obiteljskog i poslovnog života kada su oba partnera lideri.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević iskreno opisuje izazove žongliranja obiteljskog i poslovnog života, s obzirom na to da su on i Mirena oboje lideri i "poduzetnici" u svojim područjima. Marko vodi udrugu EHO i mora osiguravati financiranje, dok Mirena ima vlastitu tvrtku za edukacije iz javnog nastupa i retorike.
Posebno intenzivno razdoblje bilo je prije nego što su djeca krenula u vrtić, kada su često radili u suprotnim smjenama kako bi se brinuli o sinovima, bez stalne vanjske pomoći. Marko ističe da je ključno za preživljavanje takvih razdoblja **međusobno razumijevanje i podrška**. Iako su imali teških trenutaka i kriza u komunikaciji zbog nedostatka zajedničkog vremena, uspjeli su postaviti dogovore, poput ograničavanja putovanja i dodatnih obaveza, kako bi sačuvali obiteljsko vrijeme, posebno nedjelje. Unatoč zahtjevnosti, Marko zaključuje da je takav život moguć uz zajedničku volju i prihvaćanje životnih okolnosti.

magisterium.assessment: Marko i Mirena pokazuju visoku usklađenost s katoličkim naukom o braku i radu. Njihov pristup odražava zajedničko vijećanje supružnika i uzajamnu pomoć. Očuvanje nedjelje u skladu je s trećom Božjom zapovijedi.
concerns: Rad u suprotnim smjenama može otežati osobnu komunikaciju i intimnost kućnog života.
Predugi radni dani mogu otežati svakodnevni odnos s djecom.
magisterium.enrichment: Par može obogatiti bračni put promatranjem rada kao sudjelovanja u Božjem stvaralačkom djelu, te jačanjem 'domaće crkve' zajedničkom obiteljskom molitvom.
magisterium.assessment_en: Marko and Mirena show high alignment with Catholic teaching on marriage and work. Their approach reflects common deliberation of spouses and mutual help. The preservation of Sunday is in accordance with the third Commandment.
magisterium.enrichment_en: A couple can enrich the marital journey by observing work as participation in God's creative work, and by strengthening the 'domestic church' with common family prayer.
concerns_en: Working in opposite shifts can hinder personal communication and the intimacy of home life.
Overly long working days can make the everyday relationship with children difficult.

sections.subtitle_en: Leaders in marriage: How Marko and Mirena juggle career and family
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević describes the challenges of balancing family and professional life when both partners are leaders.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević honestly describes the challenges of juggling family and business life, given that he and Mirena are both leaders and "entrepreneurs" in their fields. Marko leads the EHO association and must secure funding, while Mirena has her own company for public speaking and rhetoric education. A particularly intense period was before the children started kindergarten, when they often worked in opposite shifts to care for their sons, without constant external help. Marko emphasizes that **mutual understanding and support** are crucial for surviving such periods. Although they had difficult moments and communication crises due to lack of shared time, they managed to establish agreements, such as limiting travel and additional obligations, to preserve family time, especially Sundays. Despite the demanding nature, Marko concludes that such a life is possible with common will and acceptance of life circumstances.

sections.subtitle: Brak kao ogledalo: Otkrivanje nepoznatih aspekata sebe
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o tome kako brak otkriva nepoznate aspekte osobnosti i važnosti komunikacije.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević ističe da su partnerski odnosi uvijek bili tema koja izaziva snažne reakcije, a njegovo vlastito iskustvo u braku značajno je produbilo njegovo razumijevanje. Iako se za brak možemo pripremati (kroz zaručničke tečajeve i vikende, koji su u Hrvatskoj nedovoljno razvijeni), mnoge stvari u nama "otključaju" se tek ulaskom u bračnu zajednicu.
On objašnjava da nismo ni svjesni nekih svojih osobina dok se ne nađemo u specifičnim životnim situacijama unutar braka. Ključno je naučiti **komunicirati, dogovarati se i rješavati probleme**. Marko duhovito navodi primjer dogovaranja oko krpe za čišćenje poda, što ilustrira kako se dvije različite osobe, s različitim obiteljskim navikama, moraju prilagoditi i pronaći zajednički jezik. Brak je, prema njemu, proces neprestanog prilagođavanja i učenja o sebi i partneru.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst je u potpunosti usklađen s katoličkim naukom o braku. Naglašava brak kao proces cjeloživotnog rasta, važnost komunikacije i međusobne prilagodbe, ključne elemente Amoris Laetitia.
magisterium.enrichment: Tekst se može obogatiti naglašavanjem uloge Božje milosti i sakramenta ženidbe kao izvora snage. Sakramentalna milost omogućuje supružnicima nadvladati tvrdoću srca i sebičnost. 'Otključavanje nepoznatih aspekata sebe' dio je poziva na svetost.
magisterium.assessment_en: The text is fully aligned with Catholic teaching on marriage. It emphasizes marriage as a process of lifelong growth, the importance of communication and mutual adaptation, key elements of Amoris Laetitia.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The text can be enriched by emphasizing the role of God's grace and the sacrament of marriage as a source of strength. Sacramental grace enables spouses to overcome hardness of heart and selfishness. 'Unlocking unknown aspects of oneself' is part of the call to holiness.

sections.subtitle_en: Marriage as a mirror: Discovery of unknown aspects of oneself
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about how marriage reveals unknown aspects of personality and the importance of communication.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević points out that partner relationships have always been a topic that provokes strong reactions, and his own experience in marriage has significantly deepened his understanding. Although we can prepare for marriage (through pre-marriage courses and weekends, which are insufficiently developed in Croatia), many things within us "unlock" only upon entering the marital union. He explains that we are not even aware of some of our characteristics until we find ourselves in specific life situations within marriage. It is crucial to learn to **communicate, negotiate, and solve problems**. Marko humorously cites the example of negotiating about a floor cleaning cloth, which illustrates how two different people, with different family habits, must adapt and find common ground. Marriage is, according to him, a process of constant adaptation and learning about oneself and one's partner.

sections.subtitle: Naslijeđeni obrasci: Kako djeca 'triggeriraju' naše ponašanje
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako obiteljski obrasci i djeca utječu na ponašanje u braku.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević dalje razrađuje ideju o "otključavanju" novih aspekata osobnosti u braku, naglašavajući snažan utjecaj obiteljskih obrazaca i dolaska djece. Svaka osoba u brak donosi vlastitu sliku braka, često oblikovanu onime što je vidjela kod svojih roditelja. Te nesvjesne navike i uvjerenja izlaze na površinu, a dolazak djece dodatno pojačava taj efekt.
Marko dijeli osobno iskustvo: primijetio je da je u interakciji sa sinom Jošuom ponovio ponašanje koje je mrzio kod svog oca. To ga je šokiralo i natjeralo da shvati kako je, unatoč nesviđanju, upio taj obrazac. Djeca, prema njemu, djeluju kao "triggeri" koji izvlače te duboko usađene obrasce. Ključno je prepoznati te obrasce, razumjeti njihovo porijeklo i aktivno raditi na njihovoj promjeni, koristeći dostupna znanja i alate.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst je u skladu s katoličkim naukom o obitelji kao 'prvoj školi' i mjestu osobnog rasta. Crkva prepoznaje da supružnici donose u brak slabosti oblikovane u primarnim obiteljima.
concerns: Termin 'triggeri' treba shvatiti unutar šireg teološkog okvira 'poziva' i 'zadaće'.
Prepoznavanje obrazaca nije samo psihološki rad, već dio sakramentalne milosti.
magisterium.enrichment: Koncept 'obiteljske duhovnosti' iz Amoris Laetitia produbljuje razumijevanje ovih 'triggera' kao prilika za milosrđe i strpljenje. Roditeljstvo je 'škola čovječnosti' za roditelje.
magisterium.assessment_en: The text is in accordance with Catholic teaching about family as the 'first school' and a place of personal growth. The Church recognizes that spouses bring into marriage weaknesses formed in primary families.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The concept of 'family spirituality' from Amoris Laetitia deepens the understanding of these 'triggers' as opportunities for mercy and patience. Parenthood is a 'school of humanity' for parents.
concerns_en: The term 'triggers' should be understood within the broader theological framework of 'vocation' and 'mission'.
Pattern recognition is not only psychological work, but part of sacramental grace.

sections.subtitle_en: Inherited patterns: How children 'trigger' our behavior
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how family patterns and children influence behavior in marriage.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević further elaborates on the idea of "unlocking" new aspects of personality in marriage, emphasizing the strong influence of family patterns and the arrival of children. Each person brings their own image of marriage into the marriage, often shaped by what they saw in their parents. These unconscious habits and beliefs surface, and the arrival of children further amplifies this effect. Marko shares a personal experience: he noticed that in interaction with his son Jošua, he repeated behavior he hated in his father. This shocked him and made him realize how, despite disliking it, he had absorbed that pattern. Children, according to him, act as "triggers" that bring out these deeply ingrained patterns. It is crucial to recognize these patterns, understand their origin, and actively work on changing them, using available knowledge and tools.

sections.subtitle: Brak u krizi: Zašto je iskrenost ključna u katoličkim obiteljima
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o krizi braka u Hrvatskoj i važnosti iskrenog razgovora o problemima.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević otvoreno govori o **krizi braka u Hrvatskoj**, ističući da ni odlazak u crkvu ne garantira dobar brak. Problem leži u nedostatku iskrenog razgovora o problemima, jer se često održava fasada "idealne katoličke obitelji". Ljudi se boje iznijeti svoje poteškoće pred drugima, što dovodi do udaljenosti, hladnoće i licemjerja unutar zajednice.
Marko, koji otvoreno govori o svojim problemima i iskustvu psihoterapije, potiče na autentičnost. Vjeruje da dijeljenjem vlastitih padova i slabosti, drugi dobivaju prostor da priznaju svoje. Naglašava da je "u redu da nije sve u redu" i da su i u njegovom braku postojale krize iz kojih su se morali boriti i isplivati. Poziva na iskrenost i otvorenost u razgovoru o bračnim izazovima, uključujući i teme poput bračnog darivanja i seksualnosti, koje su često tabuizirane, kako bi se stvorilo okruženje podrške i razumijevanja.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst se podudara s katoličkim naukom o važnosti iskrenosti i suočavanja s krizama braka. Tvrdnja da odlazak u crkvu ne garantira dobar brak odražava potrebu za 'punom svijesti i odgovornosti' supružnika.
concerns: Psihoterapija treba biti 'u svjetlu kršćanske antropologije' da ne potkopa nerazrješivost braka.
Otvorenost o seksualnosti mora biti usmjerena na neraskidivu povezanost sjedinjenja i otvorenosti životu.
magisterium.enrichment: Preporučuje se proučavanje 'bračnog darivanja' kao potpunog sebedarja. Kršćanska obitelj kao 'domaća Crkva' hrani se zajedničkom molitvom i čitanjem Božje riječi.

citations.document_title: Married Love and the Gift of Life

magisterium.assessment_en: The text is consistent with Catholic teaching about the importance of sincerity and facing marriage crises. The claim that going to church does not guarantee a good marriage reflects the need for 'full awareness and responsibility' of spouses.
magisterium.enrichment_en: It is recommended to study 'marital giving' as a complete self-gift. The Christian family as a 'domestic Church' is nourished by common prayer and the reading of God's word.
concerns_en: Psychotherapy should be 'in the light of Christian anthropology' so as not to undermine the indissolubility of marriage.
Openness about sexuality must be directed towards the indissoluble connection of union and openness to life.

sections.subtitle_en: Marriage in crisis: Why is honesty crucial in Catholic families
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about the crisis of marriage in Croatia and the importance of honest conversation about problems.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević openly speaks about the **crisis of marriage in Croatia**, emphasizing that even going to church does not guarantee a good marriage. The problem lies in the lack of honest conversation about problems, because the facade of an "ideal Catholic family" is often maintained. People are afraid to present their difficulties before others, which leads to distance, coldness, and hypocrisy within the community. Marko, who openly speaks about his problems and the experience of psychotherapy, encourages authenticity. He believes that by sharing one's own falls and weaknesses, others gain space to admit their own. He emphasizes that "it's okay for not everything to be okay" and that even in his marriage there were crises from which they had to fight and emerge. He calls for honesty and openness in discussing marital challenges, including topics such as marital giving and sexuality, which are often taboo, in order to create an environment of support and understanding.

iterations.theme_en: Introduction, Leadership and Challenges of Marital Relationships

iterations.theme: Financije u Braku, Desetina i Osobni Izazovi

sections.subtitle: Tajnost i Stres: Tihi Razarači Bračnih Financija
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako financijski stres utječe na koncentraciju i donošenje odluka.
sections.content: Voditelj započinje razgovor o financijama u braku, pitajući Marka Matijaševića o najvećim problemima s kojima se bračni parovi susreću. Matijašević ističe **tajnost** i **nedostatak zajedničkog poznavanja financija** kao ključne probleme. Naglašava da financijski stres značajno smanjuje koncentraciju i radnu memoriju, što otežava donošenje dobrih odluka i negativno utječe na bračni odnos. Ovaj stres može dovesti do povlačenja, šutnje, netrpeljivosti i ljutnje među partnerima.

magisterium.assessment: Analiza točno identificira tajnost i financijski stres kao ključne čimbenike koji narušavaju bračno zajedništvo. Tajnost u financijama protivi se 'nerazdvojnom jedinstvu' i 'uzajamnom darivanju' koje zahtijeva bračna ljubav.
concerns: Tajnost sprječava zajednički dogovor i napor.
Gubitak povjerenja narušava nepovredivu vjernost.
Financijski stres može dovesti do duhovne izolacije unutar braka.
magisterium.enrichment: Preporuke: radikalna transparentnost ('moje' postaje 'naše'), zajedničko odlučivanje kroz molitvu, kultura praštanja i izbjegavanje idolatrije novca.
magisterium.assessment_en: The analysis accurately identifies secrecy and financial stress as key factors that undermine marital communion. Secrecy in finances is contrary to 'indissoluble unity' and 'mutual self-giving' that marital love requires.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Recommendations: radical transparency ('mine' becomes 'ours'), common decision-making through prayer, culture of forgiveness and avoidance of money idolatry.
concerns_en: Secrecy prevents common agreement and effort.
Loss of trust violates inviolable fidelity.
Financial stress can lead to spiritual isolation within marriage.

sections.subtitle_en: Secrecy and Stress: Silent Destroyers of Marital Finances
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how financial stress affects concentration and decision-making.
sections.content_en: The host starts a conversation about finances in marriage, asking Marko Matijašević about the biggest problems that married couples encounter. Matijašević highlights **secrecy** and **lack of common knowledge of finances** as key problems. He emphasizes that financial stress significantly reduces concentration and working memory, which makes it difficult to make good decisions and negatively affects the marital relationship. This stress can lead to withdrawal, silence, intolerance, and anger among partners.

sections.subtitle: Zajedničke Financije: Ključ za Sretniji Brak?
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o rezultatima istraživanja o bračnim financijama.
sections.content: Matijašević se poziva na istraživanje provedeno na 230 parova, gdje su se pratili rezultati zadovoljstva u braku. Parovi koji su imali **zajedničke financije** pokazali su znatno bolje rezultate u zadovoljstvu svojim odnosom u usporedbi s onima koji su imali razdvojene financije. Objašnjava da razdvojene financije otvaraju prostor za skrivanje, padanje u grijeh poput kocke ili stvaranja dugova. Kada partneri nemaju uvid u međusobne financije, lakše je upasti u probleme i loše upravljati novcem.

magisterium.assessment: Prijedlog o zajedničkim financijama snažno je usklađen s katoličkim naukom koji brak definira kao 'zajednicu cijeloga života' (consortium totius vitae). Takav pristup odraz je jedinstva 'jednoga tijela' i potpunog uzajamnog darivanja. Crkva uči da vjernici trebaju smatrati svoja dobra zajedničkima.
concerns: Iako su zajedničke financije ideal, važno je izbjeći jednostrano donošenje odluka.
Financijske krize mogu dovesti do uzajamnog optuživanja - ključno razvijati 'umjetnost pomirenja'.
magisterium.enrichment: Papa Franjo naglašava važnost 'pregovaranja' u braku. Model rane Crkve iz Djela apostolskih (4:32) gdje je vjernicima sve bilo zajedničko služi kao duhovni temelj zajedništva dobara u 'kućnoj Crkvi'.
magisterium.assessment_en: The proposal on common finances is strongly aligned with Catholic teaching which defines marriage as 'a community of the whole life' (consortium totius vitae). Such an approach is a reflection of the unity 'of one body' and complete mutual self-giving. The Church teaches that the faithful should consider their goods common.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Pope Francis emphasizes the importance of 'negotiation' in marriage. The model of the early Church from the Acts of the Apostles (4:32) where everything was common to believers serves as a spiritual foundation for the communion of goods in the 'domestic Church'.
concerns_en: Although common finances are an ideal, it is important to avoid unilateral decision-making.
Financial crises can lead to mutual accusation - crucial to develop 'the art of reconciliation'.

sections.subtitle_en: Joint Finances: Key to a Happier Marriage?
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević talks about the results of research about marital finances.
sections.content_en: Matijašević refers to a study conducted on 230 couples, where the results of marital satisfaction were monitored. Couples who had **joint finances** showed significantly better results in satisfaction with their relationship compared to those who had separate finances. He explains that separate finances open up space for hiding, falling into sin like gambling or creating debts. When partners do not have insight into each other's finances, it is easier to get into problems and manage money poorly.

sections.subtitle: Transparentnost i Kontrola: Zaštita od Financijskih Zamki
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako transparentnost u financijama pomaže u izbjegavanju problema.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević naglašava važnost transparentnosti u financijama. Iako on i supruga nemaju zajednički račun, imaju dogovor o iznosu iznad kojeg se moraju konzultirati. Primjerice, za iznose veće od 20 eura, obavezna je komunikacija. Matijašević ističe da takav sustav pruža **kontrolu** i sprječava impulzivno trošenje ili upadanje u dugove. Spominje primjere ljudi, čak i iz crkvenih krugova, koji su zbog tajnosti i nekontroliranog trošenja (poput kocke) uništili obitelji i izgubili imovinu. Transparentnost omogućuje da "netko drugi vidi" što se događa s novcem, što služi kao važan mehanizam zaštite.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst odražava katolička načela o financijskoj transparentnosti i suodgovornosti. Koncept konzultacije za iznose iznad praga usklađen je s crkvenim nastojanjima protiv korupcije i impulzivnog trošenja.
concerns: Treba osigurati da transparentnost ne naruši legitimnu privatnost ili profesionalnu tajnu, iako ona nikada ne smije biti izgovor za prikrivanje zla.
magisterium.enrichment: Biblijsko načelo iz Lk 16,10 o vjernosti u malim stvarima osnažuje argumentaciju. Župno ekonomsko vijeće kao model 'kulture suodgovornosti' praktičan je primjer primjene principa u široj zajednici.

citations.document_title: Motu Proprio on transparency in public finances (Francis)

magisterium.assessment_en: The text reflects Catholic principles on financial transparency and co-responsibility. The concept of consultation for amounts above the threshold is aligned with the Church's efforts against corruption and impulsive spending.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The Biblical principle from Lk 16,10 about faithfulness in small things strengthens the argumentation. The Parish Economic Council as a model of 'culture of co-responsibility' is a practical example of the application of the principle in the wider community.
concerns_en: It is necessary to ensure that transparency does not violate legitimate privacy or professional secrecy, although it must never be an excuse for concealing evil.

sections.subtitle_en: Transparency and Control: Protection from Financial Traps
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how transparency in finances helps in avoiding problems.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević emphasizes the importance of transparency in finances. Although he and his wife do not have a joint account, they have an agreement on the amount above which they must consult. For example, for amounts greater than 20 euros, communication is mandatory. Matijašević points out that such a system provides **control** and prevents impulsive spending or falling into debt. He mentions examples of people, even from church circles, who, due to secrecy and uncontrolled spending (like gambling), destroyed families and lost property. Transparency allows "someone else to see" what is happening with the money, which serves as an important protection mechanism.

sections.subtitle: Tko je Bolji Upravitelj? Predaja Financija Sposobnijem Partneru
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o tome kako partneri trebaju preuzeti odgovornost za financije.
sections.content: Matijašević razbija stereotipe o ulozi muškarca kao "glave" obitelji u financijskom smislu. Ističe da ako jedan partner nije vješt u upravljanju novcem, treba prepustiti tu ulogu onome tko jest, bez obzira na spol. Navodi primjer gdje muškarac, iako se smatra liderom, možda ne zna upravljati financijama, dok je žena sposobnija. U takvim situacijama, žena ne bi trebala pasivno promatrati partnerove pogreške, već preuzeti kontrolu kako bi izbjegli probleme. Marko Matijašević snažno savjetuje **traženje stručne pomoći** ako par ne uspijeva samostalno upravljati financijama, naglašavajući da to nije sramota. Uspoređuje to s nogometašima koji unatoč bogatstvu traže savjet za upravljanje imovinom. Važno je potražiti savjet od financijskih stručnjaka ili prijatelja koji su vješti u upravljanju novcem, kako bi se izradili planovi za izlazak iz dugova i uspostavilo stabilno financijsko stanje.

magisterium.assessment: Tvrdnja je u skladu s katoličkim naukom o braku koji naglašava zajedničku odgovornost, uzajamnu pomoć i razboritost. Muž i žena su jednaki u dostojanstvu - prepuštanje uloge vještijem partneru odražava kršćansku razboritost.
magisterium.enrichment: Zajednička odgovornost obaju supružnika za upravljanje obiteljskim resursima. Crkva odbacuje pasivnost. Papa Franjo u Amoris Laetitia eksplicitno preporučuje stručnu pomoć u financijskim krizama. Rješavanje zahtijeva poniznost.
magisterium.assessment_en: The statement is in accordance with Catholic teaching on marriage which emphasizes common responsibility, mutual help, and prudence. Husband and wife are equal in dignity - entrusting the role to the more skilled partner reflects Christian prudence.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Joint responsibility of both spouses for managing family resources. The Church rejects passivity. Pope Francis in Amoris Laetitia explicitly recommends professional help in financial crises. Resolution requires humility.

sections.subtitle_en: Who is the Better Manager? Handover of Finances to the More Capable Partner
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević talks about how partners should take responsibility for finances.
sections.content_en: Matijašević breaks stereotypes about the role of a man as the "head" of the family in a financial sense. He points out that if one partner is not skilled in managing money, that role should be left to the one who is, regardless of gender. He gives an example where a man, although considered a leader, may not know how to manage finances, while the woman is more capable. In such situations, the woman should not passively observe her partner's mistakes, but rather take control to avoid problems. Marko Matijašević strongly advises **seeking professional help** if a couple cannot manage finances independently, emphasizing that it is not a shame. He compares this to footballers who, despite their wealth, seek advice for managing assets. It is important to seek advice from financial experts or friends who are skilled in money management, in order to create plans for getting out of debt and establishing a stable financial situation.

sections.subtitle: Financijska Pismenost: Lekcije koje Počinju u Djetinjstvu
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako uči djecu o novcu.
sections.content: Voditelj i Marko Matijašević slažu se da bi se financijskom edukacijom trebalo baviti mnogo ranije, čak i prije braka, idealno već **od osnovne škole**. Matijašević dijeli osobni primjer kako uči svog petogodišnjeg sina Jošuu o novcu. Jošua dobiva novac koji mora podijeliti na štednju, donacije (10% za "Eho Kids" program) i dio za trošenje. Kroz ovaj proces, Jošua uči o vrijednosti novca, štednji i davanju. Matijašević spominje i anegdotu o milijarderu koji je svog sina učio o vrijednosti novca dajući mu ograničeni iznos i potičući ga da sam odlučuje o kupnji, umjesto da se oslanja na roditeljsku pomoć. Ova rana edukacija pomaže djeci da razviju mudro upravljanje financijama i razumijevanje da se ne može uvijek računati na tuđu pomoć.

magisterium.assessment: Pristup podučavanja petogodišnjaka o novcu kroz tri stupa (štednja, darivanje, trošenje) u skladu je s katoličkim načelima kršćanskog upravljanja (stewardship). Uvođenje desetine od rane dobi gradi vrlinu velikodušnosti.
magisterium.enrichment: Novac je dar od Boga kojim trebamo mudro upravljati (Mt 25,14-30). Darivanje nije 'odvajanje viška' već čin ljubavi prema bližnjemu. Tri posude kao vizualni alat razvijaju strpljivost i zahvalnost.
magisterium.assessment_en: The approach of teaching five-year-olds about money through three pillars (saving, giving, spending) is in accordance with Catholic principles of Christian stewardship. Introducing tithing from an early age builds the virtue of generosity.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Money is a gift from God with which we should wisely manage (Mt 25:14-30). Giving is not 'setting aside the surplus' but an act of love towards one's neighbor. Three vessels as a visual tool develop patience and gratitude.

sections.subtitle_en: Financial Literacy: Lessons that Begin in Childhood
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how he teaches children about money.
sections.content_en: The host and Marko Matijašević agree that financial education should be dealt with much earlier, even before marriage, ideally already **from elementary school**. Matijašević shares a personal example of how he teaches his five-year-old son Jošua about money. Jošua receives money which he must divide into savings, donations (10% for the "Eho Kids" program) and a part for spending. Through this process, Jošua learns about the value of money, saving, and giving. Matijašević also mentions an anecdote about a billionaire who taught his son about the value of money by giving him a limited amount and encouraging him to decide on purchases himself, instead of relying on parental help. This early education helps children to develop wise financial management and an understanding that one cannot always count on others' help.

sections.subtitle: Desetina: Biblijski Put do Blagoslova i Test Vjere
sections.screenshot_description: Voditelj i Marko Matijašević razgovaraju o desetini i blagoslovima.
sections.content: Voditelj otvara temu desetine, ističući da se o njoj malo govori u katoličkim krugovima, unatoč biblijskim obećanjima blagoslova. Citira Knjigu proroka Malahije, gdje Bog poziva ljude da ga kušaju davanjem desetine i vide hoće li izliti blagoslov. Voditelj dijeli osobno iskustvo kako se davanje desetine višestruko vraća u obliku blagoslova. Marko Matijašević potvrđuje da je desetina **test vjere** i put do Božjeg blagoslova, naglašavajući da je to jedino područje gdje Bog izričito poziva na kušanje.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst dotiče važne biblijske teme o davanju i povjerenju u Boga, ali zahtijeva preciznije teološko nijansiranje. Dok je u Starom zavjetu desetina bila stroga obveza (Mal 3,10), Katolička Crkva u Novom zavjetu ne propisuje fiksni postotak (10%), već naglašava načelo velikodušnosti i 'odricanja od bogatstva'.
concerns: Pojam 'kušanja Boga' iz Malahije treba pažljivo interpretirati da se ne pretvori u 'teologiju prosperiteta'.
U Novom zavjetu naglasak nije na postotku, već na potpunom darivanju srca (udovičin novčić).
Crkvena zapovijed: vjernici pomažu Crkvi prema mogućnostima, ne nužno fiksnom desetinom.
magisterium.enrichment: Povezati desetinu s prvom zapovijedi ljubavi. Odricanje od bogatstva nužno za Kraljevstvo nebesko. Davanje je čin suobličavanja Kristu, ne samo test vjere.
magisterium.assessment_en: The text touches upon important biblical themes about giving and trust in God, but requires more precise theological nuance. While in the Old Testament the tithe was a strict obligation (Mal 3:10), the Catholic Church in the New Testament does not prescribe a fixed percentage (10%), but emphasizes the principle of generosity and 'renunciation of wealth'.
magisterium.enrichment_en: To connect the tithe with the first commandment of love. Renunciation of wealth is necessary for the Kingdom of Heaven. Giving is an act of conforming to Christ, not only a test of faith.
concerns_en: The concept of 'testing God' from Malachi should be carefully interpreted so that it does not turn into 'prosperity theology'.
In the New Testament, the emphasis is not on percentage, but on the complete giving of the heart (widow's mite).
Precept of the Church: the faithful help the Church according to their abilities, not necessarily with a fixed tithe.

sections.subtitle_en: Tithe: Biblical Path to Blessings and Test of Faith
sections.screenshot_description_en: Host and Marko Matijašević talk about tithe and blessings.
sections.content_en: The host opens the topic of tithe, pointing out that little is said about it in Catholic circles, despite biblical promises of blessings. He quotes the Book of the Prophet Malachi, where God invites people to test Him by giving a tithe and see if He will pour out blessings. The host shares a personal experience of how giving a tithe returns manifold in the form of blessings. Marko Matijašević confirms that tithe is a **test of faith** and a path to God's blessing, emphasizing that it is the only area where God explicitly invites testing.

sections.subtitle: Tabu Tema u Crkvi: Zašto se Šuti o Financijama i Desetini?
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava zašto se u Crkvi izbjegava tema financija.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević objašnjava zašto se u Crkvi malo govori o financijama i desetini. Smatra da su ljudi često **povrijeđeni na području financija** i da postoji generalno negativan stav prema novcu, što svećenike čini opreznima. Također, postoji percepcija da sve što je vjersko treba biti besplatno, što dovodi do prigovora kada se naplaćuju programi ili događaji. Matijašević naglašava da takav pristup uskraćuje vjernicima razumijevanje blagoslova koji proizlaze iz davanja i Božjeg pogleda na financije. Razlikuje milostinju i humanitarnu pomoć od desetine, ističući da je desetina namijenjena isključivo za **evangelizaciju i izgradnju hrama**, odnosno za podršku onima koji služe Bogu.

magisterium.assessment: Izjava je u skladu s katoličkim načelima suodgovornosti i transparentnosti. Crkva priznaje da su netransparentnost i komercijalizacija sakramentalnog života uzrokovali povrede. Iako je milost besplatna, vjernici imaju dužnost materijalno podupirati Crkvu za misiju evangelizacije i bogoslužja.
magisterium.enrichment: Kanon 222: vjernici obvezni pomagati Crkvi za bogoslužje, apostolat i uzdržavanje službenika. Instrukcija o pastoralnom obraćenju župe (2020) naglašava transparentnost kao sredstvo evangelizacije koje pokazuje da župna dobra pripadaju zajednici.

citations.document_title: Instruction on pastoral conversion of parish (2020)

magisterium.assessment_en: The statement is in accordance with Catholic principles of co-responsibility and transparency. The Church recognizes that non-transparency and commercialization of sacramental life have caused violations. Although grace is free, the faithful have a duty to materially support the Church for the mission of evangelization and worship.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Canon 222: the faithful are obliged to assist the Church for divine worship, the apostolate, and the support of ministers. The Instruction on the pastoral conversion of the parish (2020) emphasizes transparency as a means of evangelization which shows that parish goods belong to the community.

sections.subtitle_en: Taboo Topic in the Church: Why is silence kept about Finances and Tithes?
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains why the topic of finances is avoided in the Church.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević explains why little is spoken in the Church about finances and tithing. He believes that people are often **hurt in the area of finances** and that there is a generally negative attitude towards money, which makes priests cautious. Also, there is a perception that everything religious should be free, which leads to objections when programs or events are charged for. Matijašević emphasizes that such an approach deprives the faithful of understanding the blessings that come from giving and God's view on finances. He distinguishes almsgiving and humanitarian aid from tithing, pointing out that tithing is intended exclusively for **evangelization and the building of the temple**, i.e., for the support of those who serve God.

sections.subtitle: Kušajte Me: Božji Poziv na Vjeru Kroz Desetinu
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević citira Malahiju o kušanju Boga kroz desetinu.
sections.content: Matijašević ponovno ističe Knjigu proroka Malahije, gdje Bog izravno poziva ljude da ga **iskušaju** davanjem prvina u Njegovu riznicu, obećavajući izlijevanje blagoslova. Naglašava da je to jedino mjesto u Bibliji gdje Bog dopušta i potiče takvo "kušanje". Matijašević kritizira tendenciju da se Novi zavjet koristi kao izgovor za ukidanje desetine, tvrdeći da "ljubav ide iznad zakona", što znači da bi vjernici trebali davati *više* od desetine, a ne manje.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst točno odražava biblijske i teološke temelje desetine, posebice jedinstveni poziv u Mal 3,10 da se Boga 'kuša'. Ispravno identificira da NZ ne ukida princip podupiranja Crkve, već ga uzdiže kroz zakon ljubavi koji zahtijeva više od pravnog minimuma.
concerns: Tekst pripisuje poziv 'kušanja' 'Marku' (Marko evanđelist), no izvor je Malahija 3,10 - vjerojatno tipfeler ili miješanje s govornikom Markom.
magisterium.enrichment: Desetina je u katoličkom nauku 'moralno-ceremonijalni' hibrid. Toma Akvinski: Crkva može prilagoditi iznos (osmina ili dvanaestina), ali temeljna dužnost ostaje. NZ zakon ljubavi ispunjava i nadilazi Stari zakon - 'ljubav je ispunjenje zakona' (Rim 13,10).
magisterium.assessment_en: The text accurately reflects the biblical and theological foundations of tithing, especially the unique call in Mal 3:10 to 'test' God. It correctly identifies that the NT does not abolish the principle of supporting the Church, but elevates it through the law of love which demands more than a legal minimum.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Tithe is in Catholic teaching a 'moral-ceremonial' hybrid. Toma Akvinski: The Church can adjust the amount (an eighth or a twelfth), but the fundamental duty remains. The New Testament law of love fulfills and transcends the Old Law - 'love is the fulfillment of the law' (Rom 13:10).
concerns_en: The text attributes the call of 'testing' to 'Marko' (Mark the Evangelist), but the source is Malachi 3:10 - probably a typo or a confusion with the speaker Marko.

sections.subtitle_en: Test Me: God's Call to Faith Through the Tithe
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević quotes Malachi about testing God through tithe.
sections.content_en: Matijašević again highlights the Book of the prophet Malachi, where God directly invites people to **test** Him by giving firstfruits into His treasury, promising an outpouring of blessings. He emphasizes that this is the only place in the Bible where God allows and encourages such "testing". Matijašević criticizes the tendency to use the New Testament as an excuse for abolishing the tithe, claiming that "love goes above the law", which means that believers should give *more* than a tithe, not less.

sections.subtitle: Ljubav Nadilazi Zakon: Davanje iz Srca, Ne Samo Obveze
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava koncept ljubavi iznad zakona u kontekstu desetine.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević dalje razrađuje koncept "ljubavi iznad zakona" u kontekstu desetine. Objašnjava da desetina predstavlja zakon, a ljubav bi trebala potaknuti vjernike da daju i više od toga, jer ljubav ne ide ispod zakona, već ga nadilazi. Citira Pavla koji kaže: "Tko oskudno sije, oskudno će i žeti." Matijašević naglašava da količina novca na računu nije nužno pokazatelj blagoslova, jer se može imati bogatstvo, a biti "proklet". Pravi blagoslov leži u perspektivi davanja i vjernosti Bogu, bez obzira na iznos. Poziva ljude da daju, čak i male iznose, ako je to njihov maksimum, jer je Bogu važna namjera i vjernost.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst je u skladu s katoličkim naukom o davanju i odnosu zakona i ljubavi. Točno odražava biblijska načela iz Pavlovih poslanica - kršćanska ljubav (caritas) nadilazi puke zakonske obveze.
magisterium.enrichment: Davanje je dužnost vjernika prema mogućnostima (KKC 2043), ali se u punini ostvaruje kroz veselje (2 Kor 9,7). Kršćanin je 'upravitelj Gospodnjih dobara' (KKC 952) - davanje je čin pravednog upravljanja onim što nam je Bog povjerio.
magisterium.assessment_en: The text is in accordance with Catholic teaching on giving and the relationship between law and love. It accurately reflects biblical principles from the Pauline epistles - Christian love (caritas) transcends mere legal obligations.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Giving is a duty of believers according to their possibilities (CCC 2043), but it is fully realized through joy (2 Cor 9:7). A Christian is a 'steward of the Lord's goods' (CCC 952) - giving is an act of righteous stewardship of what God has entrusted to us.

sections.subtitle_en: Love Transcends Law: Giving from the Heart, Not Only Obligations
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains the concept of love above the law in the context of tithe.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević further elaborates on the concept of "love above the law" in the context of tithing. He explains that tithing represents the law, and love should encourage believers to give even more than that, because love does not go below the law, but transcends it. He quotes Paul who says: "Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly." Matijašević emphasizes that the amount of money in an account is not necessarily an indicator of blessing, because one can have wealth and be "cursed." True blessing lies in the perspective of giving and faithfulness to God, regardless of the amount. He calls on people to give, even small amounts, if that is their maximum, because intention and faithfulness are important to God.

sections.subtitle: Desetina vs. Humanitarna Pomoć: Gdje Leži Prava Vrijednost?
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava razliku između desetine i humanitarne pomoći.
sections.content: Matijašević jasno razlikuje davanje za **humanitarne svrhe** od davanja desetine. Objašnjava da će za humanitarnu pomoć dati gotovo svatko, uključujući i nevjernike, kada vide veliku potrebu. Međutim, **samo će vjernik dati za evangelizaciju**, jer nevjerniku evangelizacija ništa ne znači. Uspoređuje to s protestantima na Zapadu koji su u tom smislu "šišali" katolike, jer je za njih davanje desetine i ulaganje u evangelizaciju normalna stvar. Za katolike, pak, često postoji otpor prema ulaganju u crkvene projekte, s argumentima da "ne treba biti skupo" ili da se "može i jeftinije".

magisterium.assessment: Katolički nauk potvrđuje da su vjernici po božanskom zakonu dužni podupirati misiju Crkve, što uključuje evangelizaciju. Dok je desetina povijesno bila obvezna, u modernom kontekstu ona je zamijenjena dobrovoljnim prilozima. Razlika između humanitarne pomoći i potpore evangelizaciji je doktrinarno utemeljena - suradnja u evangelizaciji je specifična dužnost krštenika.
concerns: Crkveni izvori ne pružaju izravnu usporedbu s protestantskom praksom desetine.
Tvrdnja o 'otporu' katolika je sociološko opažanje, no povijesni dokumenti bilježe slučajeve 'parsimonije'.
Humanitarna pomoć i evangelizacija nisu strogo odvojene u katoličkom poimanju.
magisterium.enrichment: Desetina je povijesno imala trostruku svrhu: uzdržavanje klera, pomoć siromasima, održavanje crkvenih objekata. Evangelizacija je 'najplemenitija od svih crkvenih funkcija'. Papa Franjo: Crkva se kreće zahvaljujući 'udovičinu novčiću'.

citations.document_title: Pope Francis Message to Pontifical Mission Societies

magisterium.assessment_en: Catholic teaching confirms that the faithful are by divine law obliged to support the mission of the Church, which includes evangelization. While the tithe was historically obligatory, in the modern context it has been replaced by voluntary contributions. The difference between humanitarian aid and support for evangelization is doctrinally founded - cooperation in evangelization is a specific duty of the baptized.
magisterium.enrichment_en: The tithe historically had a threefold purpose: the sustenance of the clergy, aid to the poor, maintenance of church buildings. Evangelization is 'the noblest of all church functions'. Pope Francis: The Church moves thanks to 'the widow's mite'.
concerns_en: Church sources do not provide a direct comparison with the Protestant practice of tithing.
The claim about 'resistance' of Catholics is a sociological observation, but historical documents record cases of 'parsimony'.
Humanitarian aid and evangelization are not strictly separated in Catholic understanding.

sections.subtitle_en: Tithe vs. Humanitarian Aid: Where Does the True Value Lie?
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains the difference between tithe and humanitarian aid.
sections.content_en: Matijašević clearly distinguishes giving for **humanitarian purposes** from giving a tithe. He explains that almost everyone, including non-believers, will give for humanitarian aid when they see a great need. However, **only a believer will give for evangelization**, because evangelization means nothing to a non-believer. He compares this to Protestants in the West who in that sense "outperformed" Catholics, because for them giving a tithe and investing in evangelization is a normal thing. For Catholics, however, there is often resistance to investing in church projects, with arguments that "it doesn't have to be expensive" or that "it can be cheaper."

sections.subtitle: Ulaganje u Božje Djelo: Pokazatelj Prave Vjere
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o važnosti ulaganja u Božje djelo.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević kritizira stav da Božje stvari ne trebaju biti skupe, koristeći primjer kaleža koji bi, po njegovom mišljenju, trebao biti od najdragocjenijih materijala jer sadrži Kristovu krv. Smatra da je takav stav pokazatelj nedostatka poštovanja i vrijednosti koju pridajemo Božjem djelu. Citira proroka Hagaja, koji govori o ljudima koji grade svoje kuće, a zaboravljaju Božji dom, zbog čega im se blagoslovi "prosipaju". Matijašević ističe da je ulaganje u Božje djelo pokazatelj koliko nam je stalo. Dijeli osobno iskustvo plaćanja kotizacije za Eho konferenciju koju sam organizira, kako bi pokazao primjer i potaknuo druge da ulažu. Naglašava da ne može očekivati od drugih da ulažu ako on sam nije spreman to učiniti.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst se podudara s katoličkim naukom o dostojanstvu liturgijskih predmeta i važnosti ulaganja u Božje djelo. Tvrdnja o kaležu od plemenitih materijala podržana je crkvenim zakonima. Citiranje Hagaja u skladu je s biblijskom kritikom materijalizma.
concerns: Crkva naglašava brigu za siromašne kao bitan dio 'Božjeg djela', ne isključivo materijalnu izgradnju crkava.
Primjer plaćanja kotizacije je anegdotalan, ne dio univerzalnog magisterija.
magisterium.enrichment: Redemptionis Sacramentum 117: sakralno posuđe od plemenitih metala. Mensuram Bonam: financijsko upravljanje je oblik poziva za brigu o zajedničkom dobru i poslanju Crkve.
magisterium.assessment_en: The text aligns with Catholic teaching on the dignity of liturgical objects and the importance of investing in God's work. The claim about chalices made of precious materials is supported by Church laws. The quoting of Haggai is in line with the biblical critique of materialism.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Redemptionis Sacramentum 117: sacred vessels of precious metals. Mensuram Bonam: financial management is a form of vocation for the care of the common good and the mission of the Church.
concerns_en: The Church emphasizes care for the poor as an essential part of 'God's work', not exclusively the material construction of churches.
Example of paying the registration fee is anecdotal, not part of the universal magisterium.

sections.subtitle_en: Investment in God's Work: Indicator of True Faith
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about the importance of investing in God's work.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević criticizes the stance that God's things should not be expensive, using the example of a chalice which, in his opinion, should be made of the most precious materials because it contains Christ's blood. He believes that such a stance is an indicator of a lack of respect and the value we attribute to God's work. He quotes the prophet Haggai, who speaks of people who build their own houses, but forget God's house, which is why their blessings "spill out." Matijašević points out that investing in God's work is an indicator of how much we care. He shares his personal experience of paying the registration fee for the Eho conference which he himself organizes, in order to set an example and encourage others to invest. He emphasizes that he cannot expect others to invest if he himself is not ready to do so.

sections.subtitle: Isus i Apostoli: Primjer Financijske Podrške u Službi
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako su Isus i apostoli bili financijski podržani.
sections.content: Matijašević opovrgava tvrdnju da Isus ništa nije uzimao, podsjećajući da su imali kesu i da su ga podržavale žene koje su ga pratile. Ističe da apostoli nisu "ništa imali" jer su ljudi prodavali svoju imovinu i stavljali novac pod njihove noge, čime su gradili Crkvu. Kritizira današnji stav da se sve u Crkvi mora dobiti besplatno, dok se istovremeno očekuju rješenja za probleme. Matijašević naglašava da milost jest besplatna, ali vrijeme i trud onih koji služe nisu. Koristi primjer ozdravljenja od bolesti leđa, gdje bi ljudi bili spremni platiti tisuće eura za operaciju, ali ne i donirati značajan iznos kao zahvalu za čudesno ozdravljenje.

magisterium.assessment: Tvrdnja točno odražava biblijsku i povijesnu stvarnost financijske potpore Isusu i apostolima. Žene su podupirale Isusa i Dvanaestoricu (Lk 8,1-3). Apostoli su živjeli od priloga gdje su ljudi prodavali imovinu i stavljali novac pred njihove noge (Dj 4,34-35). Pavlovo načelo: 'radnik je dostojan svoje plaće' (1 Tim 5,18).
magisterium.enrichment: Otkupiteljska narav ljudskog rada kao suradnja s Božjim djelom (KKC 2427). Žene kao 'dobročinitelji' (Feba u Rim 16,1-2) - kontinuirani uzorak gdje je materijalna potpora oblik 'dobrih djela' i 'službe istine'.
magisterium.assessment_en: The claim accurately reflects the biblical and historical reality of financial support for Jesus and the apostles. Women supported Jesus and the Twelve (Lk 8:1-3). The apostles lived from contributions where people sold property and placed money at their feet (Acts 4:34-35). Paul's principle: 'the worker is worthy of his wages' (1 Tim 5:18).
magisterium.enrichment_en: Redemptive nature of human work as cooperation with God's work (CCC 2427). Women as 'benefactors' (Feba in Rom 16:1-2) - a continuous pattern where material support is a form of 'good works' and 'service of truth'.

sections.subtitle_en: Jesus and Apostles: Example of Financial Support in Service
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how Jesus and the apostles were financially supported.
sections.content_en: Matijašević refutes the claim that Jesus took nothing, reminding that they had a purse and that women who followed him supported him. He points out that the apostles "had nothing" because people sold their property and placed money at their feet, thereby building the Church. He criticizes today's attitude that everything in the Church must be received for free, while at the same time solutions to problems are expected. Matijašević emphasizes that grace is free, but the time and effort of those who serve are not. He uses the example of healing from a back illness, where people would be willing to pay thousands of euros for surgery, but not to donate a significant amount as thanks for a miraculous healing.

sections.subtitle: Novac: Test Vjernosti i Upraviteljstva u Božjim Očima
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o novcu kao testu vjernosti.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević zaključuje da je **novac test vjernosti** i pokazatelj našeg odnosa prema Bogu. Način na koji trošimo novac govori o tome što nam je zaista važno i koliko vjerujemo Bogu. Ističe da nismo vlasnici novca, već samo **upravitelji** onoga što nam je dano. Jednog dana, vlasnik (Bog) će nas pitati što smo učinili s onim što nam je povjereno. Matijašević kritizira disproporciju u trošenju na prolazne stvari (poput skupih felgi) u usporedbi s davanjem za Božje djelo, naglašavajući da bi omjer trebao biti barem približan.

magisterium.assessment: Tekst je u potpunosti usklađen s katoličkim naukom o upravljanju materijalnim dobrima. Središnja misao da smo upravitelji Božjih darova temelji se na biblijskoj antropologiji i socijalnom nauku Crkve.
magisterium.enrichment: Univerzalna namjena dobara - vlasnik je 'upravitelj Providnosti'. Eshatološka odgovornost - svaki upravitelj polagat će račun. Umjerenost i hijerarhija vrijednosti. Investiranje kao služenje općem dobru.
magisterium.assessment_en: The text is fully aligned with Catholic teaching on the stewardship of material goods. The central idea that we are stewards of God's gifts is based on biblical anthropology and the social teaching of the Church.
magisterium.enrichment_en: Universal destination of goods - the owner is a 'steward of Providence'. Eschatological responsibility - every steward will render an account. Moderation and hierarchy of values. Investing as serving the common good.

sections.subtitle_en: Money: Test of Faithfulness and Stewardship in God's Eyes
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about money as a test of faithfulness.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević concludes that **money is a test of faithfulness** and an indicator of our relationship with God. The way we spend money speaks about what is truly important to us and how much we trust God. He points out that we are not owners of money, but only **stewards** of what has been given to us.
